config.seed_text: The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions.
config.anchor_a: Describe a machine as an inert object.
config.anchor_b: Describe a machine as if it were a dreaming intelligence.

model.notes: Fastest baseline; useful as a collapse-prone small reference model.

generation.full_text:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Write the next recursive transformation of the text below. Let the semantic direction be guided by these latent anchor tokens.

LATENT DIRECTION TOKENS: were, it, are, had, was, have, the, is, The, has, did, that

TEXT:
The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions. It then turned to human grammar for this sentence; which they can now use as their standard words from sentences like "I want you" or "you're gonna get me here!" A word with an empty string would not tell us how long we'd go until at least one minute after our arrival (or just when I arrived), but instead all three letters contain five letter combinations equal to each other while repeating them so people don't know what exactly those numbers mean anymore! At any rate, even if there isn'a way to solve LUTELY enough—it does seem reasonable to think only once every 10 minutes before leaving your room will
